steps.title: Identify the romantic painters' approach
steps.content: The key signal is the two-part structure: romantic paintings emphasize subjects' beauty or heroism while also hiding all imperfection. This describes amplifying positive qualities to conceal flaws.

steps.title: Find the word that fits amplification
steps.content:
The blank describes what romantic paintings do to subjects' "beauty or heroism" while "hiding all imperfection." Substitute each word into "paintings which ______ their subjects' beauty or heroism":

A. Exaggerate means to represent something as larger or more extreme than it is. "Exaggerate beauty or heroism" while hiding flaws = amplifying positives and suppressing negatives -- exactly the romantic style as described. ✓

B. Correct means to fix errors or make accurate. Correcting beauty or heroism makes no logical sense -- beauty and heroism are not mistakes to be fixed.

C. Undermine means to weaken or erode. If the paintings undermined their subjects' beauty or heroism, they would be critical or negative -- contradicting "hiding all imperfection."

D. Mock means to ridicule or make fun of. Mocking subjects' beauty contradicts hiding their imperfections; mockery exposes flaws rather than concealing them.

A is correct.
